directions: Select the best option for each question

questions.text: Which of the following characteristics is the same for any electromagnetic wave traveling through empty space?

Two U.S. quarters, initially at the same temperature, are heated with a flame. One of the quarters was made before 1965 and is composed of silver. The other quarter was made after 1965 and is composed mostly of copper. What information is needed to determine which quarter will heat up faster?

answerChoices.text: The specific heat of the metals and the mass of each coin.
THe initial temperature of the coins and the mass of each coin.
The temperature of the flame and the specific heats of the metals.
The initial temperature of the coins and the temperature of the flame.

questions.text: A construction worker ensures that the air conditioner is located on the ceiling so that the cool air it pushes out falls and fills the room. Which type of heat transfer does this best represent?

A small steel rod is placed in 5 L of water. The initial temperature of the steel rod is 120 degrees Celsius, and the initial temperature of the water is 10 degrees Celsius. When does heat stop flowing between the steel rod and the water?

answerChoices.text: when the steep rod reaches 65 degrees Celsius
when the water reaches its boiling point
when the steel rod reaches 10 degrees Celsius and the water reaches 120 degrees Celsius.
when the steel rod and the water reach the same temperature

questions.text: Which of the following describes properties that distinguish red light from violet light?

answerChoices.text: Red light has a longer wavelength and a lower frequency.
Red light has a longer wavelength and a higher frequency.
Red light has a shorter wavelength and a lower frequency.
Red light has a shorter wavelength and a higher frequency.

questions.text: A book is dropped and falls to the floor. Which of the following describes the force between the book and the floor when the book hits the floor?

answerChoices.text: The book exerts a force on the floor, but the floor does not exert a force on the book.
The book does not exert a force on the floor, but the floor exerts a fore on the book.
The force exerted by the book on the floor is greater in magnitude than the force exerted by the floor on the book.
The force exerted by the book on the floor is equal in magnitude of the force exerted by the floor on the book.

questions.text: Electromagnetic radiation is used in many forms of communication. Electromagnetic waves with relatively LONG WAVELENGTHS are often used to transmit signals because these waves travel easily around objects. Which types of electromagnetic radiation is best for transmitting signals around objects?
A television produces two types of waves: light and sound. Which of the following statements describes a property of both light and sound?

answerChoices.text: They travel at the same speed.

questions.text: Which of the following describes a difference between electromagnetic and mechanical waves?

answerChoices.text: Only mechanical waves have long wavelengths?.
Only mechanical waves require a medium to travel.
Only electromagnetic waves have a range of frequencies.
Only electromagnetic waves transfer energy from one place to another.

questions.text: Which of the following phenomena best explains why light changes direction when it passes through an eyeglass lens?
The orbit of the each planet around the sun has what shape?
The next two questions deal with the electric circuit below. First: What is the total current?
What is the voltage across resistor B?
The next two questions deal with the electric circuit below. What is the current through resistor A?
What is the total resistance?
If each of the graphs below represent graphs of position-time, which one best reflects an object that is speeding up?
If each of the graphs below represent graphs of position-time, which one best reflects an object that is slowing down?
Consider the following nuclear reaction. What element is the daughter nucleus?
Consider the following nuclear reaction. What element is the daughter nucleus?
For the next two questions, consider the free-body diagram below. If the mass of the object is 4 kg, what is the magnitude of its acceleration?
What is the direction of its acceleration?

Car A has a mass of 100. kg and is moving at a speed of 7 m/s to the right. Car B has a mass of 200. kg and is moving at a speed of 4 m/s to the left. They crash into each other head-on. After they collide, Car A bounces off with a speed of 0.5 m/s to the left. Figure out what car B does:

answerChoices.text: Car B moves at 0.25 m/s to the right.
Car B moves at 0.25 m/s to the left.
Car B moves at 0.75 m/s to the right.
Car B moves at 0.75 m/s to the left.
Car B moves at 1.0 m/s to the right.
Car B moves at 1.0 m/s to the left.
